pattern: A level-order walk that enqueues children including the empty ones must hit all real nodes before any gap, so seeing a node after a gap proves incompleteness.
hints: Completeness means there is no hole followed by a later node in breadth-first order.
If you push null children into a BFS queue too, a complete tree's real nodes all come before the first null.
Run a BFS that enqueues left and right of every node (nulls included); once you dequeue a null, any later non-null node means it is not complete.

edge_inputs.description: complete tree with a partially filled last level
gap before a later node breaks completeness
missing left child but present right child
last level not packed to the left

stress.description: random level-order trees mixing complete and gapped shapes
stress.note: root always present; holes appear randomly to test both outcomes
